summary: American filmmaker known for symmetrical flat compositions, pastel color palettes, and use of Futura typeface. Major films include The Grand Budapest Hotel (2014), Moonrise Kingdom (2012), The Life Aquatic with Steve Zissou (2004), and two stop-motion features, Fantastic Mr. Fox and Isle of Dogs. Frequent collaborators include Owen Wilson, Jason Schwartzman, Adrien Brody, and Tilda Swinton.

links.text: The Life Aquatic with Steve Zissou — Wikipedia

works.name: The Grand Budapest Hotel (2014)
works.description: Anderson's most acclaimed film, set in the fictional Republic of Zubrowka. Concierge Gustave H (Ralph Fiennes) and bellhop Zero Moustafa navigate political upheaval, a disputed painting, and a murder. Based on stories by Stefan Zweig. Production design by Adam Stockhausen; the interior set was built in a mall lobby in Görlitz. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Set in the fictional Republic of Zubrowka; Ralph Fiennes plays concierge Gustave H; his bellhop is Zero Moustafa
examples: Zero Moustafa and Monsieur Gustave H are the main characters
Two characters who work in one of these places in Zubrowka are pursued by Jopling and Deputy Kovacs

clues.clue: Based on stories by Stefan Zweig; set in a country named after Hungary's capital (Budapest)
examples: is set in the fictional Republic of Zubrowka and is based on the stories of Stefan Zweig
This home country of the director of Satantango is referenced in the title of a film in which the painting Boy with Apple is inherited by a citizen of the Republic of Zubrowka.

clues.clue: The plot revolves around the painting 'Boy with Apple' bequeathed by Madame D
examples: Its plot is set into motion with the death of Madame D, who bequeaths Johannes von Hoytl's painting Boy with Apple to the protagonist.
An aerial shot depicts a woman with a Mexico-shaped birthmark hanging off a ledge on this structure by clutching the frame of a painting titled Boy with Apple.

clues.clue: Deputy Kovacs (Jeff Goldblum) is found stuffed in a sarcophagus with four fingers missing after being chased through an art museum by J.G. Jopling (Willem Dafoe)
examples: This film reveals that Deputy Vilmos Kovacs, played by Jeff Goldblum, was found 'stuffed inside a sarcophagus' with four fingers missing after a scene in which Kovacs is chased by J.G. Jopling, played by Willem Defoe, through an art museum.

clues.clue: Items are smuggled inside pink, ribboned gift boxes from Mendl's pastry shop; the film's graphic designer Annie Atkins misspelled 'patisserie' with two T's on the boxes, requiring VFX changes to nearly 3,000 boxes
examples: Many objects in this film are smuggled in pink, ribboned gift boxes from Mendl's
VFX had to alter the look of nearly 3,000 paper boxes because the film's graphic designer, Annie Atkins, misspelled a French word on them with two T's.

clues.clue: Features a first-person tandem sled chase from a monastery to a luge track
examples: A city in this country titles a film that features a first-person tandem sled chase from a monastery to a luge track.

clues.clue: The interior set was built in the lobby of a mall in Görlitz; Adam Stockhausen won an Oscar for production design
examples: The set for this structure's interior was built in the lobby of a mall in Gorlitz... Adam Stockhausen won an Oscar for production designing a film in which a shot set in this structure depicts a man running towards the frame's vanishing point.

works.name: Moonrise Kingdom (2012)
works.description: A 12-year-old Khaki Scout, Sam Shakusky (Jared Gilman), runs away with Suzy Bishop from a New England island. Features a Benjamin Britten-inspired score by Alexandre Desplat and references to Britten's opera Noye's Fludde. Stars Bruce Willis as Captain Sharp and Edward Norton as Scout Master Ward. Wikipedia

clues.clue: A 12-year-old Khaki Scout named Sam Shakusky (Jared Gilman) runs away with Suzy Bishop; they met during a performance of Benjamin Britten's opera Noye's Fludde
examples: This director of The Life Aquatic with Steve Zissou made a film about a Khaki Scout named Sam Shakusky who runs away with Suzy Bishop.
The two protagonists met after one of them sneaks into the women's dressing room during a performance of Noye's Fludde, one of the many references to Benjamin Britten in this film.

clues.clue: Captain Sharp played by Bruce Willis; Scout Master Ward by Edward Norton; Cousin Ben (Jason Schwartzman) officiates the protagonists' wedding, complaining about losing $75 in nickels
examples: New Penzance Police Captain Sharp is played by Bruce Willis and Scout Master Ward is played by Edward Norton.
Cousin Ben, played by Jason Schwartzman, complains about losing $75 in nickels while he weds the protagonists of this movie.

clues.clue: Alexandre Desplat's score was inspired by Benjamin Britten's style; Anderson himself performed in Noye's Fludde as a child
examples: This composer's style inspired Alexandre Desplat's score for Moonrise Kingdom, which was itself inspired by Wes Anderson's childhood performance in his opera Noye's Fludde.

clues.clue: Suzy injured her hand punching a mirror; Redford is stabbed in the back with left-handed scissors
examples: One of the leads in this film injured her hand when she 'lost my temper at myself' and punched a mirror. In another scene from this film, Redford is stabbed in the back with a pair of lefty scissors.

works.name: The Life Aquatic with Steve Zissou (2004)
works.description: Steve Zissou (Bill Murray) embarks on a revenge mission against a jaguar shark aboard his ship the Belafonte. Inspired by Jacques Cousteau. Features Brazilian musician Seu Jorge performing Portuguese-language David Bowie covers. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Steve Zissou is inspired by real-life oceanographer Jacques Cousteau; Cousteau also inspired Wes Anderson to make the film
examples: Wes Anderson used this man as the real-life inspiration for Steve Zissou.
Jacques Cousteau, who inspired Wes Anderson's The Life Aquatic of Steve Zissou

clues.clue: Brazilian musician Seu Jorge performs Portuguese David Bowie covers diegetically throughout the film
examples: A musician from this country frequently plays diegetic David Bowie covers in Wes Anderson's The Life Aquatic.
The soundtrack to the Wes Anderson and Noah Baumbach film The Life Aquatic is made up of this man's songs sung in Portuguese.

clues.clue: Zissou's crew is asked to join a revenge mission against a jaguar shark; someone exclaims 'I've never seen so many electric jellyfish in all my life'
examples: A character in one of his films exclaims, 'I've never seen so many electric jellyfish in all my life' shortly after being asked to join the crew of the Belafonte on a revenge mission against a jaguar shark.

works.name: Fantastic Mr. Fox (2009)
works.description: Stop-motion animated adaptation of Roald Dahl's novel. George Clooney voices Mr. Fox. Anderson used real animal fur rather than the industry-standard synthetic material. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Stop-motion adaptation of Roald Dahl's novel; Anderson used real animal fur rather than synthetic material
examples: This director notably used real animal fur in making his films, as opposed to the industry standard synthetic material. Those materials were used in his stop motion films like Fantastic Mr. Fox and Isle of Dogs.

clues.clue: George Clooney voices Mr. Fox; his only other voice role was South Park
examples: In Wes Anderson's film The Fantastic Mr. Fox, whatever the title fox says, he says with the voice of this actor. This actor's only other voice role have been in South Park.

works.name: The French Dispatch (2021)
works.description: An anthology film following four stories written for the final issue of the Liberty, Kansas Evening Sun magazine. Frances McDormand plays a journalist who helps Timothée Chalamet's teenage revolutionary write his manifesto (the 'Chessboard Revolution'/'Revisions to a Manifesto' segment). Wikipedia

clues.clue: Four employees of the Liberty, Kansas Evening Sun newspaper put together stories for its final publication
examples: One of this director's films follows four employees of the Liberty, Kansas Evening Sun newspaper as they put together stories for its final publication.

clues.clue: Frances McDormand plays a journalist who helps Timothée Chalamet's teenage revolutionary write his manifesto; he obsesses over it in the bath ('Revisions to a Manifesto' / 'Chessboard Revolution' segment)
examples: One of this director's characters, a prominent journalist, helps a teenage revolutionary write his manifesto, which he obsesses over in the bath. Those characters are played by Frances McDormand and Timothee Chalamet, respectively.
In a film by this director, a group of student protestors lead the Chessboard Revolution in a section titled 'Revisions to a Manifesto.'

works.name: Bottle Rocket (1996)
works.description: Anderson's debut feature, starring brothers Luke and Owen Wilson. The protagonist robs a bookstore with his friend Dignan and pursues a romance with a Paraguayan maid at a Texas motel. Features minor characters 'Apple Jack' and 'Future Man.' Wikipedia

clues.clue: Debut film; brothers Luke and Owen Wilson star; protagonist robs a bookstore with Dignan and pursues a Paraguayan maid at a Texas motel
examples: the brothers Luke and Owen Wilson starred in what debut film by Wes Anderson?
This film's protagonist, who is played by one of three real-life brothers, pursues a romance with a Paraguayan maid at a Texas motel after robbing a bookstore with his friend Dignan.

clues.clue: Features minor characters 'Apple Jack' and 'Future Man' (Bob Mapplethorpe's brother); first recorded instance of Owen Wilson saying 'wow' on film
examples: The first recorded instance of this happening on film was in response to a flip-book cartoon of a pole vaulter in the film Bottle Rocket.
Minor characters with odd nicknames in this film include 'Apple Jack' and 'Future Man,' the brother of a character named Bob Mapplethorpe.

works.name: General / Style
works.description: Signature style and biographical clues about Wes Anderson. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Signature style: flat, symmetric 'planimetric' shots with vibrant pastel colors; uses Futura typeface consistently
examples: This director's signature style of flat, symmetric shots with vibrant pastel colors can be observed in his film Moonrise Kingdom.
This typeface appears in the Supreme logo, Barbara Kruger's art, Wes Anderson films, and Vampire Weekend album covers.

clues.clue: Frequent collaborators: Owen Wilson (says 'wow'), Jason Schwartzman, Adrien Brody, Tilda Swinton; Mark Mothersbaugh was an earlier collaborator (Rugrats composer, DEVO)
examples: This director frequently collaborates with Jason Schwartzmann, Adrien Brody, and Owen Wilson.
This longtime collaborator with Wes Anderson produced the synthpop backbeat for 'Everything is Awesome.'

clues.clue: Designed Bar Luce, a café, for the Prada Foundation building in Milan
examples: Wes Anderson designed Bar Luce, a cafe, for this company's 'Foundation' building.

comprehensive_summary:
Wes Anderson (born 1969) is an American filmmaker known for his highly distinctive visual style: flat, symmetrical 'planimetric' compositions, vibrant pastel color palettes, and consistent use of the Futura typeface. His recurring collaborators include Owen Wilson (famously known for saying 'wow' on screen; Bottle Rocket has the first recorded instance), Jason Schwartzman, Adrien Brody, and Tilda Swinton. His earlier films were scored by Mark Mothersbaugh of DEVO. He has designed Bar Luce, a café, for the Prada Foundation.

The Grand Budapest Hotel (2014) is Anderson's most acclaimed and clued film. Set in the fictional Republic of Zubrowka (named after Budapest, Hungary) and based on stories by Stefan Zweig, it stars Ralph Fiennes as concierge Gustave H and his bellhop Zero Moustafa. The plot revolves around the painting 'Boy with Apple,' bequeathed by Madame D. Deputy Kovacs (Jeff Goldblum) is found stuffed in a sarcophagus with four fingers missing after being chased through an art museum by the villain J.G. Jopling (Willem Dafoe). Items are smuggled in pink, ribboned Mendl's pastry boxes — graphic designer Annie Atkins misspelled 'patisserie' with two T's on the boxes, requiring VFX alterations to nearly 3,000 of them. The film features a first-person tandem sled chase from a monastery to a luge track. The interior set was built in a mall lobby in Görlitz, Germany; Adam Stockhausen won the Oscar for production design.

Moonrise Kingdom (2012) follows 12-year-old Khaki Scout Sam Shakusky (Jared Gilman) and Suzy Bishop as they run away together from a New England island. They had met during a performance of Benjamin Britten's opera Noye's Fludde (Anderson himself had performed in Noye's Fludde as a child). The film is filled with references to Britten; Alexandre Desplat's score was Britten-inspired. Captain Sharp is played by Bruce Willis; Scout Master Ward by Edward Norton. Cousin Ben (Jason Schwartzman) officiates the protagonists' wedding while complaining about $75 in nickels. A lead actress injured her hand punching a mirror; another character is stabbed in the back with left-handed scissors.

The Life Aquatic with Steve Zissou (2004) was inspired by the real life of Jacques Cousteau. Steve Zissou (Bill Murray) seeks revenge on a jaguar shark aboard his ship the Belafonte. Brazilian musician Seu Jorge performs Portuguese-language David Bowie covers diegetically throughout. Fantastic Mr. Fox (2009) was a stop-motion adaptation of Roald Dahl's novel, voiced by George Clooney as Mr. Fox; Anderson used real animal fur rather than synthetic material. Isle of Dogs (2018) is his second stop-motion film, set in Japan during a canine flu outbreak.

The French Dispatch (2021) is an anthology about the Liberty, Kansas Evening Sun magazine's final issue. In the 'Revisions to a Manifesto' / 'Chessboard Revolution' segment, a journalist played by Frances McDormand helps a teenage revolutionary (Timothée Chalamet) write his manifesto, which he obsesses over in the bath. Bottle Rocket (1996), Anderson's debut feature, starred the real-life brothers Luke and Owen Wilson; the protagonist robs a bookstore with Dignan and pursues a Paraguayan maid at a Texas motel; minor characters include 'Apple Jack' and 'Future Man.'

Film: items are smuggled in pink Mendl's pastry boxes; graphic designer Annie Atkins misspelled 'patisserie,' requiring VFX changes to 3,000 boxes
Film: features a first-person tandem sled chase from a monastery to a luge track; the interior set was built in a mall lobby in Görlitz
Film: 12-year-old Khaki Scout Sam Shakusky runs away with Suzy Bishop; they met during a performance of Benjamin Britten's Noye's Fludde
Film: Cousin Ben (Jason Schwartzman) officiates the protagonists' wedding, complaining about $75 in nickels; Captain Sharp (Bruce Willis) later adopts Sam
Film: Steve Zissou (Bill Murray) seeks revenge on a jaguar shark; inspired by Jacques Cousteau; Brazilian musician Seu Jorge performs Portuguese David Bowie covers throughout

recursive_suggestions: The Grand Budapest Hotel — most frequently clued Anderson film; likely has its own dedicated tossup set. Search: 'Grand Budapest Hotel', category: Fine Arts
Moonrise Kingdom — second-most clued film with distinctive Benjamin Britten references. Search: 'Moonrise Kingdom', category: Fine Arts
